_meta.coverage_note: FULL CORPUS — all 31 May dates. May contains the feast of Sts. Cyril and Methodius (May 11, rank 3), the Prophet Jeremiah (May 1, rank 4), St. Athanasius of Alexandria (May 2, rank 3), and the Third Finding of the Head of John the Baptist (May 25, rank 2). Bright Week may fall in May; Paschal feast-rank overrides deferred per governance. Dates with unconfirmed troparion text carry troparion_status:'text-unavailable'.

commemorations.name: Holy Prophet Jeremiah

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Prophet Jeremiah
troparion.text: We celebrate the memory of Thy prophet Jeremiah, O Lord, and through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Jeremiah the Prophet (7th–6th c. BC). Rank 4. Standard prophet troparion form.

commemorations.name: St. Athanasius the Great, Archbishop of Alexandria

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Athanasius the Great
troparion.text: Among the holy Fathers thou didst shine as a great luminary of Orthodoxy; having contended for the consubstantial Trinity, thou didst put to shame the Arian heresy. O most holy Athanasius, Father of Fathers, intercede with Christ our God that He may save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Athanasius of Alexandria (+373), champion of Nicene Orthodoxy, Doctor of the Church. Rank 3. Also commemorated Jan 18 together with Cyril of Alexandria.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Timothy and Maura

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyrs Timothy and Maura
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Timothy and Maura, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Timothy and Maura (+286), martyrs of Egypt. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. Mixed-gender group uses masculine form per corpus rules. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Pelagia of Tarsus

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Pelagia of Tarsus
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Pelagia, O Lord, through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through her intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Pelagia of Tarsus (+3rd c.), virgin martyr. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, feminine form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Irene of Thessalonica

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Irene of Thessalonica
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Irene, O Lord, through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through her intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Irene of Thessalonica (+304), martyr under Diocletian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, feminine form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Righteous Job the Much-Suffering

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Righteous Job
troparion.text: We celebrate the memory of Thy righteous servant Job, O Lord, and through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Job the Righteous and Much-Suffering. Rank 4. Standard Old Testament saint troparion form.

commemorations.name: Appearance of the Sign of the Cross over Jerusalem

troparion.title: Troparion of the Appearance of the Cross over Jerusalem
troparion.text: Now the image of Your Cross has shone brighter than the sun, spreading from the Holy Mountain to the Place of the Skull, and in it O Savior, You revealed Your power, thereby strengthening us. Keep us always in peace, through the intercessions of the Theotokos, O Christ God, and save us.

commemorations.notes: Appearance of the luminous Cross in the sky over Jerusalem (351 AD), during the reign of Constantius. Rank 4. Troparion Tone 8 confirmed: OCA (May 7). Tone corrected from 1 to 8; Antiochian gives Tone 1 with different text, OCA used per source hierarchy.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian
troparion.text: O Apostle, beloved of Christ our God, hasten to deliver thy people without defense; He Who allowed thee to recline on His breast accepts thee as thou dost fall down before Him. Beseech Him, O Theologian, to dispel the persistent cloud of the nations, asking for us peace and great mercy.

commemorations.notes: John the Theologian (+ca. 100), Apostle and Evangelist. Rank 2 (Polyeleos). The repose of John is commemorated May 8; his primary feast is Sep 26.

commemorations.name: Holy Prophet Isaiah

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Prophet Isaiah
troparion.text: We celebrate the memory of Thy prophet Isaiah, O Lord, and through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Isaiah the Prophet (8th c. BC). Rank 4. Standard prophet troparion form.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle Simon the Zealot

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle Simon the Zealot
troparion.text: O Holy Apostle Simon the Zealot, intercede with the merciful God, that He grant our souls forgiveness of offences.

commemorations.notes: Simon the Zealot, one of the Twelve Apostles. Rank 3. Standard apostle troparion form (Tone 3).

commemorations.name: Equal-to-the-Apostles Cyril and Methodius, Teachers of the Slavs

troparion.title: Troparion of Sts. Cyril and Methodius
troparion.text: O holy brethren Cyril and Methodius, equal to the Apostles and teachers of the Slavs: pray to the Lord of all that He may strengthen all the Slavic nations in Orthodoxy and unity, and grant peace to the world and great mercy to our souls.

commemorations.notes: Cyril (+869) and Methodius (+885). Joint feast May 11; Cyril alone commemorated Feb 14. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: St. Epiphanius, Archbishop of Cyprus

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Epiphanius of Cyprus
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Epiphanius, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Epiphanius of Salamis (+403), Archbishop of Cyprus and defender of orthodoxy. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4 used per OCA.

commemorations.name: St. Germanus, Patriarch of Constantinople

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Germanus of Constantinople
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Germanus, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Germanus I of Constantinople (+740), defender of the veneration of icons during the first Iconoclasm. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4 used per OCA.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Glyceria

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Glyceria
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Glyceria, O Lord, through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through her intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Glyceria (+177), virgin martyr under Marcus Aurelius. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, feminine form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Isidore of Chios

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Isidore of Chios
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Isidore, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Isidore of Chios (+251), soldier-martyr under Decius. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Venerable Pachomius the Great

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Pachomius the Great
troparion.text: Thou didst forsake the cares of this life and followed Christ; having established monastic communities, thou didst lead many to salvation, O Father Pachomius. Intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Pachomius the Great (+348), founder of cenobitic monasticism in Egypt. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Theodotus of Ancyra

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Theodotus of Ancyra
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Theodotus, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Theodotus of Ancyra (+303), innkeeper and martyr under Diocletian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostles Andronicus and Junia

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostles Andronicus and Junia
troparion.text: O Holy Apostles Andronicus and Junia, intercede with the merciful God, that He grant our souls forgiveness of offences.

commemorations.notes: Andronicus and Junia — Apostles of the Seventy (Romans 16:7). Rank 4. Standard apostle troparion form (Tone 3).

commemorations.name: Venerable Peter of Caesarea in Cappadocia
commemorations.notes: Hold — monastic type. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Patrick, Bishop of Prusa, and companions

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Patrick, Bishop of Prusa, and companions
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Patrick and companions, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Patrick of Prusa (+4th c.), bishop and martyr, and companions. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Thallelaeus

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Thallelaeus
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Thallelaeus, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Thallelaeus (+284), physician-martyr under Numerian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Great Equal-to-the-Apostles Emperor Constantine and his mother Helen

troparion.title: Troparion of Sts. Constantine and Helen
troparion.text: Having seen the likeness of Thy Cross in the heavens and, like Paul, having received the calling not from men, O Lord, Thy apostle among kings, the Emperor Constantine, was set by Thy hand as king over the Roman Empire. Through the intercessions of the Theotokos, O Only Lover of mankind, keep his city in peace.

commemorations.notes: Constantine I (+337) and his mother Helena (+330), Equal-to-the-Apostles. Rank 2 (Polyeleos).

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Basiliscus of Comana

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Basiliscus of Comana
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Basiliscus, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Basiliscus of Comana (+308), companion of Theodore Tyron. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: St. Michael the Confessor, Bishop of Synnada

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Michael the Confessor of Synnada
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Michael, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Michael of Synnada (+821), confessor bishop during the second Iconoclasm. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4 used per OCA.

commemorations.name: Venerable Simeon the Stylite the Younger

troparion.title: Troparion of the Venerable Simeon the Stylite the Younger
troparion.text: Dweller of the desert and angel in the body, you were shown to be a wonder-worker, our God-bearing Father Simeon. You received heavenly gifts through fasting, vigil, and prayer: healing the sick and the souls of those drawn to you by faith. Glory to Him who gave you strength! Glory to Him who granted you a crown! Glory to Him who through you grants healing to all!

commemorations.notes: Syrian stylite (+592) on the Wonderful Mountain near Antioch; not to be confused with Simeon the Elder (Sept 1). Tone 1 confirmed OCA May 24 troparia page (direct fetch). Queue held Tone 1 — correct.

commemorations.name: The Third Finding of the Head of John the Baptist

troparion.title: Troparion of the Third Finding of the Head of the Baptist
troparion.text: The memory of the righteous is celebrated with praise, but the Lord's testimony is sufficient for thee, O Forerunner; for thou hast proved to be truly more venerable than the Prophets, since thou wast granted to baptize in the stream Him Whom they proclaimed. Wherefore, having contested for the truth, thou didst rejoice to announce to those in Hades the good tidings of God Who had appeared in the flesh; Who takes away the sin of the world, and grants us great mercy.

commemorations.notes: Third Finding of the Head of John the Baptist. Rank 2 (Polyeleos). Same Troparion as Feb 24 First and Second Finding (Tone 4).

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle Carpos

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle Carpos
troparion.text: O Holy Apostle Carpos, intercede with the merciful God, that He grant our souls forgiveness of offences.

commemorations.notes: Carpos, one of the Seventy Apostles, companion of Paul (2 Timothy 4:13). Rank 4. Standard apostle troparion form (Tone 3).

commemorations.name: St. John the Russian, Confessor

troparion.title: Troparion of St. John the Russian, Confessor
troparion.text: He Who called you from earth into the heavenly abodes keeps your body incorrupt even after your death, O righteous John; for you were taken as a prisoner into Asia where you also won Christ as your friend. Therefore, entreat Him that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: John the Russian, confessor and wonderworker. Rank 4. Proper troparion Tone 4 confirmed from OCA and Antiochian witnesses. The prior combined entry with Constantine-Cyril was corrected to John the Russian alone for this date.

commemorations.name: St. Nikita (Niketas), Bishop of Chalcedon

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Niketas of Chalcedon
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Nicetas, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Niketas (Nikita) of Chalcedon (+9th c.), confessor bishop under Iconoclasm. Rank 4. Troparion Tone 4 confirmed: OCA (May 28).

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Theodosia of Caesarea in Cappadocia

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Theodosia of Caesarea in Cappadocia
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Theodosia, O Lord, through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through her intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Theodosia of Caesarea (+308), virgin martyr under Maximinus. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, feminine form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Venerable Isaakios, Abbot of the Dalmatian Monastery
commemorations.notes: Hold — monastic type. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Hermias

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Hermias
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Hermias, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Hermias (+2nd c.), soldier-martyr under Antoninus Pius. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.
